steps.title: Count total vertices and favorable outcomes
steps.content: A 13-sided polygon has 13 vertices, each labeled with a different letter from A through M. When we select one vertex at random, there are 13 equally likely outcomes. Exactly one of these vertices is labeled with the letter H.

steps.title: Apply the probability formula
steps.content:
The number of favorable outcomes is 1 (the vertex labeled H). The total number of equally likely outcomes is 13 (all the vertices).
$$P = \frac{1}{13}$$
